archetype: Offshore Carbon Sequestration Site Seismic Integrity Verification System (OCSS-SIVS)
domain: Marine Geophysics for Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS)
description: A real-time system that processes continuous 4D time-lapse seismic data from ocean-bottom node (OBN) arrays to detect micro-seismic events and fluid migration anomalies within subsea carbon storage reservoirs, used by geophysicists and regulatory auditors to verify containment integrity. It's technically hard due to massive petabyte-scale time-series seas, low-bandwidth acoustic telemetry from seabed sensors, and the need to distinguish injection-induced micro-seismicity from natural tectonic noise and tidal strain noise.
rules: MUST ingest and process raw seismic data in SEG-Y rev2 format with precise navigation (lat/lon/depth) and timing (GPS-synched) metadata from multi-vendor OBN equipment.
MUST implement OGC SensorThings API for harmonizing disparate node manufacturer data streams and provide a standardized observation feed for external audit tools.
MUST comply with ISO 14001:2015 environmental management and OSPAR Convention guidelines for subsea storage monitoring, generating immutable audit logs for all model runs and parameter changes.

MUST support automated detection of 'bright spots' (fluid accumulations) and 'velocity pull-down' (pressure changes) in repeated 3D seismic volumes against a high-fidelity baseline survey, using co-registration algorithms tolerant to tidal and ocean-current-induced baseline shift.

MUST provide a secure, standards-based (WFS/WMS) geospatial portal for regulators to visualize time-lapse difference cubes, event catalogs, and uncertainty metrics without requiring licensed commercial interpretation software.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use generic cloud object storage (e.g., S3) as primary data lake without a dedicated seismic-optimized parallel file system (like Ceph with BeeGFS) due to catastrophic performance degradation for small, random seismic trace reads.

DO NOT apply standard statistical anomaly detection without domain-specific seismic attributes (e.g., semblance, curvature) and rock physics templates, as it will generate overwhelming false positives from ocean noise and gas chimneys unrelated to injection.
DO NOT design a batch-only processing pipeline; regulatory 'alarm' conditions for pressure-boundary breach require sub-hour latency from seabed acquisition to interpreted risk flag on the dashboard.
key_technical_challenges: Real-time co-registration of time-lapse 3D seismic volumes acquired with different OBN node geometries and coupling conditions across multiple survey vessels, requiring precise velocity model updates and non-linear warping algorithms.

Building a scalable, low-latency compute pipeline for full-waveform inversion (FWI) velocity model updates that can run on a hybrid HPC/cloud cluster while managing the extreme I/O of 10s of TB per survey.

Maintaining sensor calibration drift compensation across thousands of autonomous OBNs over a 5-year project lifecycle, using cross-node consistency checks and periodic 'kicker' air-gun calibration shots.

recommended_stack.language: C++/Python hybrid: C++ for high-performance seismic I/O and processing kernels (SEG-Y libraries), Python for orchestration, machine learning (PyTorch/TensorFlow), and API layer.
recommended_stack.framework: FastAPI with async support for high-throughput seismic metadata and alert APIs; Dask for parallel geophysical computation orchestration.
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB for structured time-series metadata (node health, acquisition parameters) and PostGIS for spatial queries on seismic geometry.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Cluster for caching frequently accessed baseline volumes, velocity models, and real-time alert state to reduce HPC cluster load.
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka for buffering high-volume raw seismic trace chunks and event detections from the field acquisition system to the processing farm.
special_services: OpenDTect or SeisSpace (via licensed API) for core seismic processing and attribute calculation as a microservice.
Custom Geotiff/COG (Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF) generation service for OGC-compliant difference cube visualization.
HDF5 with chunked, compressed layout for efficient storage and partial reads of massive 4D seismic volumes.

compliance_flags: EU Directive 2009/31/EC on Geological Storage of CO2 (or national equivalent like US EPA Class VI)
ISO 27914:2017 (Carbon dioxide capture, transportation, and geological storage)
IEC 61508 SIL 2 for safety-critical alarm systems if linked to injection well ESD (Emergency Shutdown) logic
